title: Argentine government removes import tariff on electric and hybrid vehicles
bodyHtml:
The Argentine government has confirmed the removal of the 35% import tariff on electric and hybrid vehicles, permitting up to 50,000 units per year for a five-year period.

The procedure for setting the import duty rate on electric vehicles to zero has been approved by the Secretariat of Industry and Trade of the Ministry of Economy of Argentina through Decree No. 49/2025 and Resolution No. 29/2025 dated February 28, 2025. The vehicles covered by these regulations are specified in Articles 1 and 2 of the aforementioned Decree and Article 4 of Resolution No. 29/2025.

In this context, it has been deemed appropriate to issue a call for applications for the electric vehicle import quota under Decree No. 49/2025 and Resolution No. 29/2025.

The allocation will be made in two categories: 25,000 units for terminal companies based and operating in Argentina and 25,000 units for individuals or legal entities that do not meet these conditions.

According to Decree No. 30/2025, published in the Official Gazette of Argentina (https://www.boletinoficial.gov.ar/detalleAviso/primera/322609/20250317), the Ministry of Economy, through the National Directorate of Industrial Policy Management, has opened the call for applications for the allocation of an import quota of up to 50,000 electric vehicles for 2025. The vehicles must have the specific technologies outlined in Articles 1 and 2 of Decree No. 49/2025 and Article 4 of Resolution No. 29/2025, and their maximum FOB unit value must not exceed USD 16,000.

Vehicles eligible under this scheme must meet specific technical requirements, such as weighing over 400 kg (excluding the battery), having a minimum power output of 15 kW and a range exceeding 80 km.

The quota distribution will be as follows:

  • 25,000 units allocated to companies with production plants established in Argentina.
  • 25,000 units for importers without local manufacturing.

Allocation will be based on the date of vehicle nationalisation and the lowest price offered by the bidding companies.

The tariff positions covered under the Argentine tariff schedule are:

8703.40.00

8703.50.00

8703.60.00

8703.70.00

8703.80.00
